title: Woodworking Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Except Sawing
description: Set up, operate, or tend woodworking machines, such as drill presses, lathes, shapers, routers, sanders, planers, and wood nailing machines. May operate computer numerically controlled (CNC) equipment.

tasks.text: Set up, program, operate, or tend computerized or manual woodworking machines, such as drill presses, lathes, shapers, routers, sanders, planers, or wood-nailing machines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine finished workpieces for smoothness, shape, angle, depth-of-cut, or conformity to specifications and verify dimensions, visually and using hands, rules, calipers, templates, or gauges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start machines, adjust controls, and make trial cuts to ensure that machinery is operating properly.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor operation of machines and make adjustments to correct problems and ensure conformance to specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Examine raw woodstock for defects and to ensure conformity to size and other specification standards.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adjust machine tables or cutting devices and set controls on machines to produce specified cuts or operations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install and adjust blades, cutterheads, boring-bits, or sanding-belts, using hand tools and rules.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Change alignment and adjustment of sanding, cutting, or boring machine guides to prevent defects in finished products, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Determine product specifications and materials, work methods, and machine setup requirements, according to blueprints, oral or written instructions, drawings, or work orders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Feed stock through feed mechanisms or conveyors into planing, shaping, boring, mortising, or sanding machines to produce desired components.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Push or hold workpieces against, under, or through cutting, boring, or shaping mechanisms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select knives, saws, blades, cutter heads, cams, bits, or belts, according to workpiece, machine functions, or product specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove and replace worn parts, bits, belts, sandpaper, or shaping tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Secure woodstock against a guide or in a holding device, place woodstock on a conveyor, or dump woodstock in a hopper to feed woodstock into machines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inspect and mark completed workpieces and stack them on pallets, in boxes, or on conveyors so that they can be moved to the next workstation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Inspect pulleys, drive belts, guards, or fences on machines to ensure that machines will operate safely.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Clean or maintain products, machines, or work areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attach and adjust guides, stops, clamps, chucks, or feed mechanisms, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Trim wood parts according to specifications, using planes, chisels, or wood files or sanders.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Grease or oil woodworking machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Unclamp workpieces and remove them from machines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Start machines and move levers to engage hydraulic lifts that press woodstocks into desired forms and disengage lifts after appropriate drying times.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate gluing machines to glue pieces of wood together, or to press and affix wood veneer to wood surfaces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up, program, or control computer-aided design (CAD) or computer numerical control (CNC) machines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Control hoists to remove parts or products from work stations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
